name: Overleaf
description: An online LaTeX editor with real-time collaboration, built-in compilation, and integration with journal submission systems. Google Docs for physicists.
whyUseIt: Overleaf lets multiple authors edit a LaTeX document simultaneously, with instant preview and no local installation required. It stores version history, integrates with Git, and provides direct submission to major physics journals.
fieldPosition: Universal — collaborative scientific writing and paper preparation
physicistWorkflow: Create a project from a journal template, write collaboratively in real time, compile and review, and submit directly to the journal.
supervisorExpects: Professional manuscripts prepared collaboratively with proper formatting and version history.
computationalImpact: Eliminates LaTeX installation issues, email-based document passing, and merge conflicts in collaborative writing.

vocabulary.definition: A collection of LaTeX files that compile into a single document
A WYSIWYG-like editing mode for authors unfamiliar with LaTeX syntax

beginnerMistakes: Not using Overleaf's built-in Git sync for backup
Editing directly in the PDF preview instead of the source code
